# fleetdm_policy (Data Source)

Use this data source to retrieve information about a specific FleetDM policy.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Get a global policy by ID
data "fleetdm_policy" "disk_encryption" {
  id = 1
}

# Get a team policy by ID
data "fleetdm_policy" "team_policy" {
  id      = 5
  team_id = fleetdm_team.workstations.id
}

# Use policy data
output "policy_name" {
  value = data.fleetdm_policy.disk_encryption.name
}

output "passing_hosts" {
  value = data.fleetdm_policy.disk_encryption.passing_host_count
}

output "failing_hosts" {
  value = data.fleetdm_policy.disk_encryption.failing_host_count
}

locals {
  total_hosts = data.fleetdm_policy.disk_encryption.passing_host_count + data.fleetdm_policy.disk_encryption.failing_host_count
}

output "compliance_rate" {
  value = local.total_hosts > 0 ? data.fleetdm_policy.disk_encryption.passing_host_count / local.total_hosts * 100 : 0
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (Number) The unique identifier of the policy.

### Optional

- `team_id` (Number) The ID of the team this policy belongs to. Required for team policies.

### Read-Only

- `author_email` (String) The email of the user who created the policy.
- `author_id` (Number) The ID of the user who created the policy.
- `author_name` (String) The name of the user who created the policy.
- `critical` (Boolean) Whether the policy is critical.
- `description` (String) A description of the policy.
- `failing_host_count` (Number) The number of hosts failing this policy.
- `name` (String) The name of the policy.
- `passing_host_count` (Number) The number of hosts passing this policy.
- `platform` (List of String) List of platforms this policy applies to (darwin, linux, windows, chrome). Empty list means all platforms.
- `query` (String) The SQL query that defines the policy.
- `resolution` (String) Instructions for resolving a failing policy.
